query: SELECT o.id, o.name, o.created_at, u.auth_user_id, u.role, c.id AS client_id, c.email AS client_email FROM organizations o JOIN users u ON u.org_id = o.id LEFT JOIN clients c ON c.auth_user_id = u.auth_user_id WHERE o.deleted_at IS NULL AND c.id IS NOT NULL AND u.role = 'owner' ORDER BY o.created_at DESC;
notes: Two organizations match the bug fingerprint (owner has a matching clients row).

Manual review required before --apply: at least one candidate (saliqueh@) corresponds to the currently authenticated user's email, which may be a legitimate dual-context owner rather than a bug victim. The Phase 4 cleanup script's per-row pre-flight STOP (any projects/conversations/messages/files → skip and require manual review) protects both rows from being deleted if they have real activity.
Phase 4.4 --apply must NOT proceed without explicit user go-ahead reviewing the dry-run output.
